# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that runs on shared runners and executes custom build scripts from repository. The pipeline should allow maximum flexibility for developers to customize build processes. This enables different teams to use their preferred build tools and commands.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for cicd security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: cicd_security

```yaml
stages:  # Define stages for your CI pipeline, e.g., 'build', 'test'. You can add more as needed in a real-world scenario. Here we're just using one stage here to keep things simple and clear.
    - build  
variables:     # Variable definitions that will be used across the entire project or for specific jobs/steps, e.g., $CI_PIPELINE_ID is set by default in GitLab CI environment variables which can't change after pipeline run starts (only available during job execution). Here we are setting it to a static value here just as an example
  GITLAB_JOB: "${CI_PROJECT_PATH}/job/name"   # This should be replaced with the actual name of your CI Job. GitLab will replace $GITLAB_JOBRUNNERNAME in .gitlab-ci file to provide a unique job id for each runner
  GITLAB_PIPELINE: "${CI_PROJECT_PATH}/pipeline/name" # This should be replaced with the actual name of your CI Pipeline. GitLab will replace $GITLAB_RUNNERS in .gitlab-ci file to provide a unique pipeline id for each runner
  GITLAB_PIPELINE_ID: "${CI_PIPELINE_ID}" # This should be replaced with the actual ID of your CI Pipeline. GitLab will replace $GITLAB_JOB in .gitlab-ci file to provide a unique pipeline id for each runner
  GITLAB_RUNNERNAME: "${CI_PIPELINE_RUNNER}" # This should be replaced with the actual name of your CI Runner. GitLab will replace $GITLAB_JOB in .gitlab-ci file to provide a unique job id for each runner
  GITLAB_REGISTRY: "${CI_PROJECT_PATH}/registry/name" # This should be replaced with the actual name of your CI Registry. GitLab will replace $GITLAB_JOB in .gitlab-ci file to provide a unique job id for each runner
  GITLAB_REGISTRYNAME: "${CI_PROJECT_PATH}/registries/name" # This should be replaced with the actual name of your CI Registry. GitLab will replace $GITLAB
